concerns.text: FR-012 requires comparing XGBoost top-3 features against the *significant* coefficients from the LME model. T033 compares against *all* coefficients (absolute values) regardless of p-value significance. This misses the specific constraint in FR-012 to filter by statistical significance before comparison.

FR-009 mandates a sensitivity analysis repeating the *mixed-effects fit* across α ∈ {0.01, 0.05, 0.10}. T026 describes repeating the LME fit, but T030/T031 apply LOAFO/stratified splits to the *predictive* (XGBoost) model. There is no explicit task to re-run the LME model (US-2) with the three different alpha thresholds to generate the required variation report for SC-003.

FR-005 requires dropping/combining predictors if *any* predictor has VIF > 5. T023 only implements the logic for Energy Density VIF > 5 (dropping components). It lacks the reciprocal task: if components are retained but *they* have VIF > 5, the system must drop/combine them. This leaves a gap in the collinearity resolution workflow defined in FR-005.

FR-001 requires merging Materials Project descriptors into the unified dataset. T016 fetches and merges but lacks a verification step to ensure the *merged* dataset still meets the row count (≥50) and column integrity requirements of US-1. A silent join failure could result in an empty dataset without triggering the US-1 warning logic.

FR-010 and US-3 define a 'held-out test set' separate from the 5-fold CV used for tuning. T030 implements LOAFO as the *test set* strategy for XGBoost when N < 100. This conflates the validation strategy (CV) with the final evaluation strategy (held-out), potentially violating the spec's requirement for a distinct held-out set for final metrics (R², MAE, RMSE).

T042 introduces a 'robustness check' to proceed with a warning if VIF > 5 persists after filtering. This contradicts the strict imperative in FR-005 ('System MUST drop or combine predictors... to resolve collinearity'). While T042 addresses an edge case, the task description weakens the 'MUST' constraint of the spec, creating a potential compliance gap.
